content:
The architecture that gives you microservices benefits without the microservices pain.

15 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Every engineering team faces the same fork in the road. Your monolith is growing. Features are tangled. Deployments are risky because touching one module breaks three others. The consultants say: “Go microservices. Break it apart. Embrace the chaos.”
So you do. You spend 18 months extracting services. You build a Kubernetes cluster. You add service meshes, distributed tracing, circuit breakers, and event buses. Your infrastructure bill triples. Debugging a request now requires querying 7 different logs. A simple feature requires deploying 4 services in the right order. Your best engineers spend more time on DevOps than features.
Then someone on Twitter says: “Have you tried a modular monolith?”
A modular monolith is the architecture nobody talks about because it’s not buzzword-compliant. It’s not a monolith — your modules are properly isolated with strict boundaries. It’s not microservices — your modules live in one deployable unit. You get the development velocity of a monolith with the organizational clarity of services.
This article is based on real production experience. By the end, you’ll have:
